questions.question: What is a common challenge in training neural networks with large datasets?
questions.explanation: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and performs well on the training data but poorly on new, unseen data.

questions.question: What is a technique used to regularize neural network weights to prevent overfitting?
questions.explanation: Regularization involves adding a penalty term to the loss function to discourage large weight values.

questions.question: Why is it difficult to optimize neural network weights using gradient-based methods?
options: A) Because of non-differentiable activation functions
questions.explanation: Gradient-based methods can struggle with vanishing gradients (small gradients that are difficult to update) and exploding gradients (large gradients that can cause the model to overshoot the optimal solution).

questions.question: What is a common challenge in training neural networks with complex objectives?
questions.explanation: Complex objectives can lead to mode collapse (the model converging to a single solution instead of exploring the full range of possibilities).

questions.question: Why is it difficult to optimize neural network weights using reinforcement learning?
options: A) Because of the need for exploration
B) Because of the need for exploitation
C) Because of the need for balancing exploration and exploitation
questions.explanation: Reinforcement learning requires balancing exploration (trying new actions to learn about the environment) and exploitation (choosing actions that maximize the cumulative reward).
